note: Simplenews has a three-entity model (newsletter config entity, subscriber content entity, issue = node with a field), spool+cron sending, double opt-in, a subscription-manager service, a recipient-handler plugin type, and six permissions. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) probing documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: In Simplenews on Drupal 11, what is the difference between a newsletter, a subscriber, and a newsletter issue, and what entity/type is each?
evals.expected_output: A newsletter is a simplenews_newsletter config entity (a list/category); a subscriber is a simplenews_subscriber content entity keyed by email; an issue is a node whose content type has the simplenews_issue field.

evals.prompt: How do I make a content type able to be sent as a Simplenews newsletter issue?
evals.expected_output: Attach the simplenews_issue field to the content type (via the content type edit form); each node of that type then becomes an issue with a Newsletter tab to send/test.

evals.prompt: How does Simplenews actually send a newsletter issue to subscribers — describe the spool and cron flow and the two services involved.
evals.expected_output: Sending an issue writes one spool row per recipient via simplenews.spool_storage (addIssue); simplenews.mailer->sendSpool() drains it during cron (mail.throttle mails per run) or immediately when mail.use_cron is FALSE.

evals.prompt: Which Simplenews settings control whether mails are sent during cron and how many go out per cron run? Give the config object and keys.
evals.expected_output: On simplenews.settings: mail.use_cron (default TRUE) sends the spool during cron; mail.throttle (default 20) is the number of mails per cron run.

evals.prompt: In PHP on a Drupal 11 site, subscribe an email address to a Simplenews newsletter programmatically and check whether it is already subscribed. Which service and methods?
evals.expected_output: Use the simplenews.subscription_manager service: subscribe($mail, $newsletter_id, $langcode) to subscribe and isSubscribed($mail, $newsletter_id) to check.

evals.prompt: How does Simplenews handle double opt-in for anonymous subscribers, and which setting disables the confirmation step?
evals.expected_output: Anonymous subscribers are created UNCONFIRMED and emailed a hash-authenticated confirmation link before becoming ACTIVE; subscription.skip_verification (default FALSE) on simplenews.settings disables the confirmation.

evals.prompt: In Simplenews, how do I control which subscribers receive a given newsletter issue, and what plugin type / default plugin is involved?
evals.expected_output: Recipients are chosen by a simplenews_recipient_handler plugin; the default simplenews_all sends to all active subscribers. Implement a custom handler or restrict via the newsletter's allowed_handlers.

evals.prompt: Which Simplenews permission lets front-end visitors subscribe to newsletters, and which one gates sending newsletter issues?
evals.expected_output: 'subscribe to newsletters' allows front-end subscribing (block/subscriptions page); 'send newsletter' gates sending/queuing issues. (Admin gated by 'administer newsletters' / 'administer simplenews subscriptions' / 'administer simplenews settings'.)
